title: [VPlan] Remove unused VPExpandSCEVRecipe before expansion
author: Mel-Chen
description: VPExpandSCEVRecipe may become unused after VPlan optimizations. This patch removes VPExpandSCEVRecipes with no users before expansion in expandSCEVs, avoiding generating dead code during VPlan execution.

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define void @test_start_zext(i32 %start, ptr %dst) {
entry:
  %start.ext = zext i32 %start to i64
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv.0 = phi i64 [ %start.ext, %entry ], [ %iv.0.next, %loop ]
  %iv.1 = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.1.next, %loop ]
  %gep.dst = getelementptr float, ptr %dst, i64 %iv.1
  store float 0.000000e+00, ptr %gep.dst, align 4
  %iv.1.next = add i64 %iv.1, %start.ext
  %iv.0.next = add i64 %iv.0, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv.0.next, 100
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define void @strided_ptr_iv_runtime_stride(ptr %pIn, ptr %pOut, i32 %nCols, i32 %stride) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %in.ptr = phi ptr [ %pIn, %entry ], [ %in.next, %loop ]
  %out.ptr = phi ptr [ %pOut, %entry ], [ %out.next, %loop ]
  %val = load float, ptr %in.ptr, align 4
  store float %val, ptr %out.ptr, align 4
  %in.next = getelementptr inbounds float, ptr %in.ptr, i32 1
  %out.next = getelementptr inbounds float, ptr %out.ptr, i32 %stride
  %iv.next = add nuw i32 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %iv.next, %nCols
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

declare void @foo()

; Function Attrs: mustprogress
define void @expand_diff_scev_unknown(ptr %dst, i1 %invar.c, i32 %step) #0 {
entry:
  br label %loop.1

loop.1:                                           ; preds = %loop.1, %entry
  %iv.1 = phi i32 [ %step, %entry ], [ %iv.1.next, %loop.1 ]
  call void @foo()
  %iv.1.next = add i32 %iv.1, 1
  br i1 %invar.c, label %loop.2, label %loop.1

loop.2:                                           ; preds = %loop.2, %loop.1
  %iv.2 = phi i32 [ %iv.1, %loop.1 ], [ %iv.2.next, %loop.2 ]
  %iv.2.next = add nsw i32 %iv.2, %step
  %gep.dst = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %dst, i32 %iv.2
  store i32 0, ptr %gep.dst, align 4
  %ec.2 = icmp slt i32 %iv.2.next, 0
  br i1 %ec.2, label %loop.2, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.2
  ret void
}

attributes #0 = { mustprogress }

define void @fn1(ptr noalias %x, ptr noalias %c, double %a) {
entry:
  %conv = fptosi double %a to i32
  %conv2 = add i32 %conv, 4
  %cmp8 = icmp sgt i32 %conv2, 0
  br i1 %cmp8, label %loop, label %exit

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %0 = trunc i64 %iv to i32
  %mul = mul nsw i32 %0, %conv
  %mul.ext = sext i32 %mul to i64
  %gep.x = getelementptr inbounds double, ptr %x, i64 %mul.ext
  %1 = load double, ptr %gep.x, align 8
  %gep.c = getelementptr inbounds double, ptr %c, i64 %iv
  store double %1, ptr %gep.c, align 8
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %iv.trunc = trunc i64 %iv.next to i32
  %ec = icmp eq i32 %iv.trunc, %conv2
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:o-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

; Function Attrs: mustprogress
define void @zext_of_i1_stride(i1 %g, ptr %dst) #0 {
entry:
  %g.16 = zext i1 %g to i16
  %g.64 = zext i1 %g to i64
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds i16, ptr %dst, i64 %iv
  store i16 %g.16, ptr %gep, align 2
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, %g.64
  %cmp = icmp ult i64 %iv.next, 16
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

attributes #0 = { mustprogress }

target datalayout = "e-m:o-i64:64-i128:128-n32:64-S128"
target triple = "arm64-apple-macosx14.0.0"

define void @s172(i32 noundef %xa, i32 noundef %xb, ptr noundef %a, ptr noundef %b) {
entry:
  %sub = sub nsw i32 %xa, 1
  br label %for.cond

for.cond:                                         ; preds = %for.inc, %entry
  %i.0 = phi i32 [ %sub, %entry ], [ %add3, %for.inc ]
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %i.0, 32000
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.cond.cleanup

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.cond
  %idxprom = sext i32 %i.0 to i64
  %gep.b = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %b, i64 %idxprom
  %l.b = load i32, ptr %gep.b, align 4
  %idxprom1 = sext i32 %i.0 to i64
  %gep.a = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %a, i64 %idxprom1
  %l.a = load i32, ptr %gep.a, align 4
  %add = add nsw i32 %l.a, %l.b
  store i32 %add, ptr %gep.a, align 4
  br label %for.inc

for.inc:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  %add3 = add nsw i32 %i.0, %xb
  br label %for.cond, !llvm.loop !0

for.cond.cleanup:                                 ; preds = %for.cond
  br label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.cond.cleanup
  ret void
}

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.mustprogress"}

comments.author: jplehr
comments.body: I believe this breaks one of our bots: https://lab.llvm.org/buildbot/#/builders/123/builds/36163/steps/11/logs/stdio

comments.author: jplehr
comments.body: Reverting this patch locally brings back success in the test suite.
